message:
chore(gitignore): ignore /data/ and /logs/ indexer working dirs + .beads/*.lock

* /data/ and /logs/ at the repo root are regenerated on demand by
  `cass index` runs (the data/index/ Tantivy shard + data/index-run.lock,
  and a rolling per-run log set under logs/). Previous benchmark
  sessions left these behind as untracked, cluttering git status and
  tempting `git add .` into committing transient index state. These
  directories are never inputs to anything — the canonical state is
  `~/.local/share/coding-agent-search/agent_search.db` and the
  per-test-case `data_dir` passed on the CLI.

* .beads/*.lock covers .beads/.write.lock and .beads/.sync.lock, which
  now appear during any mutating `br` operation or `br serve` session
  after beads_rust 3a40ad0 landed the MCP-side write-lock lifecycle.
  The existing .beads/.bv.lock line only covered the bv advisory lock.

files.patch:
@@ -35,6 +35,10 @@ ubs-report.*
 parse_ubs.py
 fix_*.py
 
+# Local indexer working dirs and log output (regenerated on demand)
+/data/
+/logs/
+
 # One-off test source files
 test_empty_db.rs
 
@@ -133,6 +137,7 @@ user_requests_output.txt
 
 # Beads ephemeral files (locks, migration hints, backups)
 .beads/.bv.lock
+.beads/*.lock
 .beads/.migration-hint-ts
 .beads/*.bak_*
